pipeline_status.poller: QUIET — 10th consecutive quiet poll. No substantive changes to dome site detected. GitHub API not accessible (rate-limited), no commit data. All 7 pages checked, V51.0 parameters unchanged. Eclipse prediction (Aug 12, 2026: -17 to -21 nT) still featured.
pipeline_status.analyst: Globe Fingerprint Hunt (Mode 3). No pending expansions, no pending human notes. WIN-015 null result (no globe fingerprint — zero computational content, bare metadata citation). Globe fingerprint hunt: 15/67 complete, 52 remaining.
pipeline_status.curmudgeon: Cycle 2, Phase 1: 22/99 items reviewed (WIN-001 through WIN-022). Last review: WIN-022 (1990 magnetic phase transition). Verdict Misleading confirmed with increased confidence 0.85→0.92. WIN-022.c2.json marked as processed.

external_changes.dome_site_changes: No changes detected. 10th consecutive quiet poll. V51.0 with 67 WINs, 95.2% accuracy claim unchanged.

integrity_status.external_links: WARN — 1 broken: INTERMAGNET data download page (https://www.intermagnet.org/data-donnee/download-eng.php, 404 after site redesign). Source in wins.json WIN-042: intermagnet.org/data_download.html

internal_issues.description: INTERMAGNET data download link 404: intermagnet.org/data_download.html returns 404 after INTERMAGNET website redesign. Integrity check identified on 2026-04-06T21:21Z. Source link in WIN-042 detail_evidence field.
CHAOS-7 DOI error: current DOI 10.1186/s40623-020-01163-9 is wrong, should be 10.1186/s40623-020-01252-9 (Olsen et al. 2020). Unfixed from Cycle 1; curmudgeon alerts.txt flagged as CRITICAL UNFIXED.
internal_issues.note: Patch already written in prior run. Awaiting human application.

internal_issues.description: Wrong Terra-Nova DOI. Current link doi.org/10.1016/j.epsl.2017.06.036 redirects to wrong paper.
internal_issues.note: Patch written in prior run. Awaiting human application.

internal_issues.description: Spatial heterogeneity loophole not addressed. The dome can escape the κ argument by claiming different stations have different local values.
WIN-054 verdict mismatch: current verdict is 'Not Demonstrated' but should be 'Misleading' — the dome is cherry-picking a tension that has published explanations.
internal_issues.note: Patch in prior run. Awaiting human application.

internal_issues.description: The dome's evolution page explicitly argues WIN-002 and WIN-029 are the SAME prediction (26% damping from aetheric coupling), making them non-independent.
internal_issues.note: EXP-013 integrated. Patch in prior run.

internal_issues.description: Numerator (-10.9 nT, BOU 2017) and denominator (-6.5 uGal, Mohe 1997) are from different stations, different years, different events — the ratio κ = 1.67 is not a measured physical constant.
Review does not address dome defender's ratio stability argument: if κ is stable across many events, the cross-station/cross-event mixing is irrelevant.
WIN-011: relabels_standard tag is 'true' but should be 'false'. Standard physics (EIGEN-6C4) predicts the gravity anomaly but does NOT relabel the mechanism.
WIN-013: monitoring tag is 'hardcoded' but should be 'none'. WIN-013 is NOT validated by monitor.py Domain #6 — Domain #6 measures SAA decay at Tsumeb, unrelated to Membach SG.

WIN-005 Cycle 2: PROS-005 prospective test is retroactive. WMM2025 data was publicly available since late 2024; the dome registered the prediction March 6 and confirmed it March 12 — a 6-day turnaround.
EXP-013 incomplete integration: expansion-tracker.json marks EXP-013 as integrated for WIN-002 but open-issues.json ISS-479 (the source issue) still shows status 'patched' rather than being closed.
internal_issues.note: Patch in prior run addressing both ISS-479 and ISS-530.

internal_issues.description: WIN-020 Cycle 2: code_analysis.derives_from_dome tag is true but should be false. The 18.6-yr cycle is derived from standard mechanics, not dome geometry.
WIN-021: code_analysis.monitoring tag is 'hardcoded' but should be 'none'. monitor.py has no domain that validates gyroscopic precession rate.
WIN-021: finding punchier — current finding buries the lede. Should lead with 'Globe value passed as dome prediction.'
WIN-021: current text says 'across 39 domains' which is inaccurate after monitor.py corrections.

holes_found.description: Minor terminological clarification on second-order phase transitions. Already implicitly addressed by current text. No action needed.
Optionally add Chulliat & Newitt (2010, EOS 91(49)) citation alongside Livermore et al. (2020) for completeness.
holes_found.action: no_issue_created — optional enhancement, not a weakness

recommended_actions.action: Apply 21 outstanding patch files. CRITICAL: ISS-490 (CHAOS-7 DOI) and ISS-513 (Terra-Nova DOI) are citation errors that damage credibility. The curmudgeon alerts.txt explicitly flags ISS-490 as CRITICAL UNFIXED. Use: node build-scripts/apply-patches.js <patch-file>
Apply ISS-536 patch (broken INTERMAGNET link in WIN-042). Included in suggested-patches-2026-04-07T22-00.json.
Sync code_analysis tags: run 'node build-scripts/sync-code-analysis.js --apply --workspace' after applying WIN-011/013/020/021 code_analysis patches. Several tags updated in Cycle 2 reviews.
After patches applied, rebuild and publish: node build.js publish

notes: Poller: GitHub API inaccessible on latest run — potential rate-limiting. Not yet persistent enough to flag as infrastructure issue (only 1 run). Monitor.
Curmudgeon: progressing normally. Cycle 2 Phase 1 at 22/99. WIN-022.c2.json processed this run.
Analyst: Mode 3 globe fingerprint hunt operating normally. 15/67 complete.
All 16 EXP items complete and integrated — no pending expansions.
21 patch files await human application — this backlog is the main operational concern.

summary: Quiet run. No new dome site changes, no unintegrated analyst expansions, no unpatched critical/major issues remaining from prior curmudgeon reviews. WIN-022 Cycle 2 review processed — verdict Misleading confirmed with increased confidence (0.92), no actionable issues. New issue ISS-536 created and patched for broken INTERMAGNET data link found by integrity agent. The main action item remains applying 21 outstanding patch files.
